name: MSW (Mock Service Worker)

agent_readiness.notes: Network-level HTTP/GraphQL mocking using Service Worker (browser) or Node.js interceptors. http.get('/api/user', resolver) / graphql.query('GetUser', resolver). Same handlers for browser and Node tests. Works with fetch, axios, @apollo/client. REST and GraphQL handlers. Passthrough for real requests. One-time mock with { once: true }. Response delay: delay(500). Error simulation: HttpResponse.error(). Testing Library + MSW is the modern React testing stack.

gotchas: v2 has breaking changes from v1 (new API surface)
Browser requires Service Worker registration (public/ directory)
GraphQL mocking requires exact query matching
Server component testing requires Node.js mode
best_when: React/Next.js component testing with realistic API mocks
Integration tests that need to mock HTTP without coupling to implementation
Sharing mock handlers between unit and integration tests
avoid_when: When WireMock provides better team-shared contract mock server
summary: MSW intercepts HTTP at network level using Service Workers (browser) or Node.js interceptors. Same handlers for browser and Node tests. REST and GraphQL support. Works with Testing Library, Vitest, Jest. http.get('/api/endpoint', ({ request }) => HttpResponse.json(data)). The modern standard for React/Next.js component testing. Replaces axios-mock-adapter and fetch-mock.
